answer.text: In this toy transistor-switch model, a low input leaves the transistor in cutoff (OFF) and a high input drives it into saturation (ON), so the load behaves like an on/off branch rather than a linear amplifier.

reason_why.text: We model an NPN low-side switch with exact millivolt and microamp arithmetic. The base current comes from (Vin - Vbe)/Rb when the base-emitter junction is forward biased, and the collector current is the smaller of beta * Ib and the load-limited current (Vcc - Vce,sat)/Rl.

reason_why.label: low input
reason_why.value: Vin=0.00 V -> Ib=0.00 mA, Ic=0.00 mA, Vce=5.00 V, state=cutoff / OFF

reason_why.label: high input
reason_why.value: Vin=5.00 V -> Ib=0.43 mA, Ic=4.80 mA, Vce=0.20 V, state=saturation / ON
